8: Autonomous Networks: AI systems are capable of analyzing alerts and
9: forecasting network issues and traffic anomalies by leveraging key performance
10: indicators (KPIs) and identified patterns. Nevertheless, crucial decisions--such as
11: addressing high-risk alerts--are frequently escalated to human analysts. These
12: analysts conduct further investigation and make the ultimate determination
13: regarding the approval of network changes.
14: This pattern exemplifies a practical method for AI implementation. It harnesses AI for

17: "Human-on-the-loop" is a variation of this pattern where human experts define the
18: overarching policy, and the AI then handles immediate actions to ensure compliance.
21: Automated financial trading system: In this scenario, a human financial expert
22: sets the overarching investment strategy and rules. For instance, the human
23: might define the policy as: "Maintain a portfolio of 70% tech stocks and 30%
24: bonds, do not invest more than 5% in any single company, and automatically sell
25: any stock that falls 10% below its purchase price." The AI then monitors the stock
26: market in real-time, executing trades instantly when these predefined conditions
27: are met. The AI is handling the immediate, high-speed actions based on the
28: slower, more strategic policy set by the human operator.
30: Modern call center: In this setup, a human manager establishes high-level
31: policies for customer interactions. For instance, the manager might set rules such
32: as "any call mentioning 'service outage' should be immediately routed to a
33: technical support specialist," or "if a customer's tone of voice indicates high
34: frustration, the system should offer to connect them directly to a human agent."
35: The AI system then handles the initial customer interactions, listening to and
36: interpreting their needs in real-time. It autonomously executes the manager's
37: policies by instantly routing the calls or offering escalations without needing
